# Transcrição: 8. Find Your Music

- **Duração:** 01:26

## [00:00 - 01:26]

Alright, look, you gotta find your music. You gotta find that thing that slam jams bumps you to get you excited, to get you jacked up. I don't care what it is. It's rap. Turn it down. It's rap. It's country. Whatever turns you on. But man, stay away from the blues. The blues ain't gonna help you on the phone today, okay? I don't want to go back and talk about the losses of my life. The relationship that went bad. I need to be talking about something that gets me thinking about jamming, hustling, working. Whatever music gets you like, you know, that music can get you like, I can't stop myself. I can't even dance. But I'm feeling like I want to do the bump. Like whatever music you need, okay? Look, you're listening to the news. You're listening to Anderson Cooper. You're listening to O'Reilly. You're listening to the weather. How is the weather reporter Anderson Cooper O'Reilly or Rush gonna help you today? It's not, man. Get you some music, man. Get you some music about the hustle. Get you some music going that vibrates through this core of you and wakes you up. What have you done today already? You woke up two hours early. You beat the sun up. That's awesome. You cleaned up your space. You got rid of all the old garbage you don't need. You got rid of all the distractions. You got rid of the past, right? You did your workout this morning. You wrote your goals down. You got your eye on the prize. You got your workout done now. You need to do, oh yeah, man. Turn that music on, man. Let's roll, okay? You got to get it going. Yeah.
